answer:
The harp can play glissandos only on scales or chords that use each of the seven diatonic pitches once per octave, without repeated notes or half-step gaps that require changing finger placement. The C whole-tone scale (①) and chromatic scale (⑤) are impossible because they require repeated notes or half steps that cannot be smoothly glissed. The Ab Ionian mode (②), a minor harmonic D-VII7 chord (③), and G major D7 chord (④) all fit the harp’s tuning for a clean glissando.

\boxed{D}
